hero.title: Academic Integrity
hero.subtitle: Educational platform that detects plagiarism with AI, executes code securely in Docker containers, and provides complete traceability of the development process.

features.title: Everything you need to evaluate code securely
features.subtitle: From plagiarism detection to execution in isolated containers.

plagiarism.title: AI-Powered Plagiarism Detection
plagiarism.description: Semantic code analysis using language models to detect similarities beyond syntax.

docker.title: Secure Execution in Docker
docker.description: Each code runs in isolated, ephemeral containers with resource limits, ensuring complete security.

traceability.title: Complete Traceability
traceability.description: Records every student action: keystrokes, time spent, and code changes in real-time.

lti.title: LTI 1.3 Integration
lti.description: Native connection with Canvas LMS. Grades sync automatically without manual intervention.

pytest.title: pytest Evaluation
pytest.description: Teachers define unit tests in pytest. The system automatically runs and evaluates each submission.

realtime.title: Real-Time Editor
realtime.description: Students write code directly in the browser with syntax highlighting and autocomplete.

challenge.title: Academic Integrity Without Compromise
challenge.subtitle: Generative AI has changed the game in education.
challenge.problemText: Students can generate complete code with ChatGPT in seconds, making it impossible to verify the actual authorship of the work.
challenge.solutionText: Complete traceability of the development process: every keystroke, every pause, every modification is recorded. We don't just evaluate the final code, but the entire thought process behind it.

stack.title: Built with modern and reliable technologies
stack.subtitle: A robust architecture that ensures security, scalability, and performance.

traceability.title: Every action tells a story
traceability.subtitle: Turn development data into insights about the learning process.

keystroke.title: Keystroke Recording
keystroke.description: Captures every keystroke, including copy-paste, allowing identification of code copied from external sources.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.description: Visualize code evolution minute by minute. Detect suspicious pauses or sudden massive changes.

heatmap.title: Heat Map
heatmap.description: Identify which parts of the code the student spent the most time on, revealing areas of difficulty or possible copying.

analysis.title: Pattern Analysis
analysis.description: ML algorithms detect inconsistent writing patterns suggesting AI use or copied code.

tutorials.subtitle: Step-by-step guides to start evaluating Python code with academic integrity.

tutorials.ctaText: Need more help? Check out our complete documentation.

faq.title: Have questions? We're here to help
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about Edurun.

lti.question: How does Canvas integration work?
lti.answer: Edurun natively implements the LTI 1.3 (Learning Tools Interoperability) protocol. This means students access Edurun directly from Canvas without needing to log in separately. Grades obtained in Edurun automatically sync with Canvas gradebook. Initial setup takes approximately 10 minutes following our installation guide.

ai.question: How do you detect AI use like ChatGPT?
ai.answer: We don't rely on a single method. We combine: (1) Writing pattern analysis: detect abrupt changes in style or speed suggesting copy-paste of AI-generated code. (2) Semantic comparison: compare student code with typical ChatGPT/Copilot patterns. (3) Temporal traceability: complex code appearing suddenly without previous iterations is highly suspicious. The system generates a confidence report that the teacher interprets.

languages.question: Do you only support Python?
languages.answer: Currently, yes. We focus on Python because it's the most widely used language in introductory programming and data science courses. However, the architecture is designed to be language-agnostic. Support for JavaScript, Java, and C++ is on our 2026 roadmap.

security.question: Is it safe to execute student code?
security.answer: Absolutely. Each code runs in an isolated, ephemeral Docker container with limited resources (CPU, memory, execution time). Containers have no network access or access to the host file system. After execution, the container is automatically destroyed, ensuring complete security.

cloud.question: Do I need to install anything on my computer?
cloud.answer: No. Edurun is a 100% cloud-based platform. Both teachers and students access from the web browser without needing to install any software. Only an internet connection and a modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge) are required.
